attempt.claim: Erdos #686: lean/Vela/Erdos686.lean (kernel-checked, axiom-clean, no sorry) proves the k=2 and k=4 obstructions for prime-power squares. If A is a prime power then A^2 has no representation N=prod_{i<=k}(m+i)/prod_{i<=k}(n+i) (m>=n+k) with k=2 or k=4 -- in particular N=4 has none -- via prime-power divisibility (no multiple of A lies strictly between consecutive multiples A*N0 and A*(N0+1)) plus a general k=4 => k=2 reduction with the same multiplier ((t+1)..(t+4)=4*(u+1)(u+2)). This is theorem-grade partial progress; it does NOT address k=3, k>=5, or composite non-prime-power square N.
